title: 7.3.5 Evaluation Procedures for Out-of-Plane Behavior of Wall and Pier Components
scope: Prescriptive strength and deformation acceptance criteria for out-of-plane wall demands.

immediate_occupancy.requirement: Flexural stresses must not exceed the tensile capacity of the wall.
immediate_occupancy.compliance_logic: Any level of damage above 'Insignificant' (where flexural cracking has occurred) implies the wall does not meet this performance level.

life_safety_and_collapse_prevention.standard_reference: FEMA 273 (ATC, 1997a) Table 7-4 provides permissible h/t ratios for walls without prior damage.
life_safety_and_collapse_prevention.factor_source: Component Guides (e.g., URM1M) specify the required lambda_h_t-factors based on damage severity.

in_plane_interaction.guideline: The Component Guide provides additional lambda-factors for instances where a wall with out-of-plane damage is also acting as a component in a force-deformation curve parallel to its length (in-plane loading).
